methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $24M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Emerald AI is a seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Varun Sivaram that has raised $24.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), including advanced materials and energy storage players such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems operating in adjacent technology segments.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $24.5M total raised as a proxy for current valuation, applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $61M. The peer-median method examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A)—establishing a median valuation of $150M with a range of $110M to $200M.

These methods triangulate to a low of $52M, median of $106M, and high of $172M. The stage-progression result anchors the lower bound, while the peer median reflects recent capital efficiency in climate tech but carries material uncertainty given Emerald AI's pre-series-A status relative to Series A comparables. Key variables affecting valuation include near-term funding trajectory, technical de-risking milestones, and relative market positioning within an increasingly crowded climate tech landscape. The $52M–$172M range reflects this inherent uncertainty at seed stage.
